title: Partnership; Christ proclaimed; to live is Christ
summary_text: Paul thanks God for Philippians’ fellowship in gospel from first day; confident he who began work will complete. Paul’s bonds advance gospel; brethren bold. Christ preached—envy rivalry or love. To live Christ, to die gain; fruitful labor strait between.
key_points: I thank my God whenever I remember you.
Being confident of this very thing, that he who began a good work in you will complete it.
Now I desire to have you know that the things which happened to me have turned out rather to the progress of the Good News.
Christ is proclaimed, and I rejoice in this.
For to me to live is Christ, and to die is gain.
evidence_phrases: I thank my God
he who began a good work in you
the things which happened to me
Christ is proclaimed
to live is Christ, and to die is gain

title: Humility of Christ; shine as lights
summary_text: Be likeminded; lowliness—Christ though equal with God emptied, humbled to death on cross; God highly exalted him. Work out salvation with fear—God works in you. Without murmuring; shine as lights; Timothy and Epaphroditus commended.
key_points: Let this mind be in you, which was also in Christ Jesus.
He emptied himself, taking the form of a servant.
God also highly exalted him, and gave to him the name which is above every name.
Work out your own salvation with fear and trembling, for it is God who works in you.
You shine as lights in the world.
evidence_phrases: Let this mind be in you
He emptied himself
every knee should bow
God who works in you
you shine as lights in the world

title: Loss counted rubbish; heavenly citizenship
summary_text: Beware dogs, evil workers—circumcision of heart. Paul’s gains counted loss for excellency of knowing Christ; righteousness from God through faith. Press toward mark; citizenship in heaven—we await Savior who will transform body.
key_points: Beware of the dogs, beware of the evil workers.
I count all things to be loss for the excellency of the knowledge of Christ.
That I may gain Christ and be found in him, not having a righteousness of my own.
I press on toward the goal.
Our citizenship is in heaven. From there we also wait for a Savior.
evidence_phrases: Beware of the dogs
I count all things to be loss
that I may know him
not having a righteousness of my own
I press on toward the goal
our citizenship is in heaven

title: Rejoice; peace; thanks; provision
summary_text: Rejoice in Lord always; gentleness known; peace of God guards hearts. Think on whatever true, honorable, just. Learned contentment in any state—I can do all things through Christ who strengthens. God shall supply; greet every saint.
key_points: Rejoice in the Lord always! Again I will say, ‘Rejoice!’
The peace of God, which surpasses all understanding, will guard your hearts.
Whatever things are true, honorable, just, pure, lovely—think about these things.
I have learned in whatever state I am, to be content in it.
I can do all things through Christ, who empowers me.
evidence_phrases: Rejoice in the Lord always
The peace of God
think about these things
I have learned … to be content
I can do all things through Christ
My God will supply every need of yours
